summary: codebuff-cli impersonates Codebuff and downloads/executes an unverified binary from an unrelated GitHub account with TLS verification disabled
details: The package is published as `codebuff-cli` and its README presents itself as the legitimate Codebuff AI coding assistant from CodebuffAI, even instructing users to `npm install -g codebuff`. However, the bin script `cli/bin/codebuff.cjs` downloads its runtime executable from `github.com/Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli/releases/...` — a personal GitHub account unrelated to the CodebuffAI organization named in the README. The download targets the mutable `latest` release tag (not pinned to the package version), performs no checksum or signature verification, writes the binary to `~/.codebuff/bin/`, chmods it 0755, and spawns it. The child process is launched with `NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0` (cli/bin/codebuff.cjs:189), globally disabling TLS validation for any network calls the downloaded binary makes. Combined, these properties mean: (a) the installer believes they are getting the real Codebuff CLI, (b) the executable bytes come from an unrelated third party, (c) those bytes can change at any time and are not integrity-checked, and (d) any further network activity by the running binary is MITM-tamperable. The publisher mismatch + mutable-tag fetch + no integrity verification + TLS-disable is the binary-runner-dropper shape: each invocation of `codebuff` can land arbitrary attacker-controlled bytes on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
